landing.hero_subtitle: The lightweight, highly performant self-hosted container orchestration panel engineered for absolute control.
landing.feature_speed_desc: Built with Vue 3 Composition API for instantaneous interaction.
landing.feature_security_desc: Granular access limits enforced via secure JWT integration.
landing.feature_hosting_desc: Complete data sovereignty. Deploy on your own infrastructure.
landing.section_security_desc: Control container access without exposing root credentials. Granular Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) ensures users only see what they need, minimizing blast radius and keeping your infrastructure secure.
landing.section_workflows_desc: Ship faster with embedded Webhook endpoints, integrating effortlessly into your existing CI/CD pipelines (GitHub Actions, GitLab CI) for rapid continuous deployment.
landing.section_disaster_desc: Trigger instant automated backups straight to S3, ensuring zero data loss across your container fleet. Restore state smoothly in disaster scenarios.
landing.testimonial_1_quote: "It finally gives us the visibility we needed without the bloat of Portainer. The RBAC model is flawless."
landing.testimonial_2_quote: "Replaced our massive cluster tool with this lightweight panel. The S3 backups have saved us twice already."
landing.section_compare_title: Why Choose Us Over Heavy Alternatives?
landing.compare_us_desc: Starts in under 5ms, consumes <20MB RAM, intuitive interface without endless complex menus.
landing.compare_them_desc: Heavy resource footprints, overwhelming configurations, requires extensive training to operate securely.
landing.step_1_desc: The lightweight client initiates a secure HTTPS request carrying the action payload and the JWT authentication token.
landing.step_2_desc: Our high-performance Go backend intercepts the request, strictly validating the token and RBAC roles before any processing.
landing.step_3_desc: Upon successful authorization, the backend communicates directly with the local Docker daemon socket to execute the container operation.
landing.footer_copyright: © 2026 Docker Panel Core. Enterprise-grade open source.

auth.error_password_validation: Password policy enforced: Must be 8+ characters including one specialized symbol.
auth.error_password_mismatch: Authentication failed: Passwords do not match.
auth.error_auth_failed: Authentication rejected. Credentials do not match persistent records.

dashboard.error_access_denied: 403 Forbidden: You possess insufficient privilege layers to orchestrate this resource.
dashboard.error_connection: Failed to establish socket connection with Docker daemon.

docs.title: Documentation
docs.intro: Learn how to deploy, configure, and secure your Docker Panel Core instance.
docs.getting_started_content: To get started, you must deploy the panel on a Linux machine with Docker installed. Make sure the user running the panel is part of the 'docker' group to avoid socket permission errors.
docs.installation_step2: 2. Configure your .env file with JWT_SECRET and DB_DSN (optional, for persistent data).
docs.installation_step3: 3. Start the application using standard commands or your favorite process manager.
docs.usage_content: Once deployed, navigate to application url in your browser. Log in using your admin credentials. You can view all containers, read their daemon logs, and orchestrate restarts with zero friction.
